captured_ideas_summary: Key ideas and follow-ons already captured durably in Airtable: collector contextual help and command rendering, review-surface tuning, chunking/pathway/default-lane validation, Gemini startup/truthfulness/query safety/provenance/version behaviors, and plan-task global key normalization to avoid cross-plan overwrite.
decisions_constraints: Do not treat repo work as fully complete until collector actions/workflows pass. buffered_promotion_candidates: No additional buffered GitHub promotion candidates remain from this session beyond workflow-result updates after collector action runs. Repo-change tasks for the pushed batches were marked done in Airtable where implemented.
next_recommended_move: Run the collector GitHub action/workflow suite on the current repo, then update Validation Test Cases and any still-open plan tasks from the observed results.
resume_prompt: Resume from CHK-20260424-SESSION-CLOSEOUT-001. Treat repo follow-through as complete for the pushed batches. Keep Gemini live evaluation deferred to next week; do not mark Gemini fully validated yet.

fields.event_summary:
idea_id: IDEA-20260423-GEM-NORESULT-HONESTY-GATE
idea_title: Gemini no-results honesty gate
idea_detail: Add a durable Gemini validation/repair rule that when analyst-run KQL/ESQL/osquery steps return no results, Gemini must treat that as bounded absence rather than converting it into proof of stealth, malware, or collapse of the benign hypothesis. Require the response to explicitly separate: what the alert directly proves, what the no-result query fails to add, and what coverage limitations could still explain the gap.
why_it_matters: This transcript shows a reusable failure pattern: Gemini escalated from negative telemetry into strong maliciousness language and a collector pivot without enough direct evidence. Preserving the rule will help future repair and validation work stay evidence-first.
related_area: Gemini
status: promoted_to_plan
notes: Derived from 2026-04-23 live alert-JSON transcript. Candidate downstream targets: negative-result honesty lane GEM-NEGATIVE-001, support-proof lane, and output-contract analysis guidance.

fields.event_title: IDEA-20260422-GH-SHA-MISMATCH-RECOVERY
fields.payload_json: {"created_at": "2026-04-28T11:12:38.000Z", "idea_detail": "During live Gemini governed-source repair, a GitHub `update_file` attempt failed with a 409 SHA mismatch because the file had advanced beyond the earlier fetched snapshot. The correct recovery was not to treat this as a mysterious blocker. The right bounded recovery is: refetch the exact live file from `main`, take the new blob SHA as authoritative, rebase the prepared edit onto that current content, and retry the write against the refreshed SHA. Treat this as a normal connector write-race or stale-snapshot condition rather than a workflow derailment.", "idea_id": "IDEA-20260422-GH-SHA-MISMATCH-RECOVERY", "idea_title": "GitHub update_file SHA mismatch should trigger immediate live-state refetch and reapply, not confusion or delay", "notes": "Observed and recovered during 2026-04-22 Gemini runtime cleanup source-edit lane. Desired future behavior: immediately refetch live file state, refresh SHA, reapply bounded edit, and continue without treating the mismatch itself as a hard blocker.\nPromoted by DCOIR Airtable Massive Architecture Package massive-v4 at 2026-04-28T18:59:35+00:00: plan_id=PLAN-IDEA-idea-20260422-gh-sha, work_item_id=WI-FROM-IDEA-idea-20260422-gh-sha-mis", "retention_class": "review", "review_after": "2026-04-28", "status": "promoted_to_plan", "suggested_promotion_target": "GitHub task-memory / blocker-recovery procedure candidate", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T18:59:35.000Z", "why_it_matters": "This is a reusable GitHub connector blocker-recovery lesson for governed existing-file updates. Capturing it should reduce future hesitation, prevent stale-sha confusion, and support faster recovery in grouped GitHub write lanes."}

The correct recovery was not to treat this as a mysterious blocker. fields.event_summary: Implemented durable guardrails to prevent future chunk/parts drift in the DCOIR chatgpt-apply-in lane. GitHub workflow now rejects chunk/parts markers and decodes only a single payload.zip.b64. Staging README documents the contract. Repo builder/validator creates one payload.zip.b64 only. Core parity runbook, operator tools README, and tool catalog now point to the single-payload builder. Airtable Operator Tools Registry, dcoir-memory-preflight SKILLROUTE, dcoir-decision-policy hard-stop, Validation Test Cases, and Validation Evidence now carry the rule. Optional extra reject-only workflow for root part-*.b64 files was attempted but connector blocked creation of an intentionally failing workflow; current main workflow still rejects parts when a payload run is selected and the docs/tools forbid all parts/chunks.

fields.event_title: Recorded guardrail coverage for invalid ChatGPT apply-in staging shapes
fields.payload_json: GitHub commit edd174811bdebfdcd3110c5b77cd546a06e111bc; workflow path .github/workflows/chatgpt-apply-in-invalid-staging-cleanup.yml; readback SHA 31f791f0e0cb436bc00cea3ea730cefb79fb3066; validation evidence record recK4PoaXPsgQfOLR.
fields.event_summary: Added a GitHub workflow guardrail for unsupported ChatGPT apply-in staging shapes under chatgpt_staging/in. The guardrail watches for part-style or chunk-style staging paths, records a status report, and directs future sessions back to the approved single payload.zip.b64 contract. This closes the previously noted gap where an invalid standalone staging file might not have been covered by the primary apply-in workflow trigger.

plan_title: Promoted idea: Gemini attachment review must state exact IOC provenance
plan_state: draft
scope_constraints: Add a reusable Gemini workflow rule for report-driven IOC expansion/removal: when the analyst uploads incident documents and asks Gemini to add missing IOCs or remove incident-localized ones, Gemini must enumerate the files it actually reviewed, distinguish analyst-supplied IOCs from report-derived IOCs, and give short provenance notes for each added or removed indicator family. why_current_task_matters: This transcript showed a support-proof gap: analyst note said 5 documents were attached, but Gemini claimed review while naming only 3 and then changed the IOC set without visible per-file grounding. Preserving this as a durable rule helps future Gemini evidence review and repair work.

fields.event_summary:
plan_id: PLAN-IDEA-idea-20260424-airtab
plan_title: Promoted idea: Create Airtable row-delete trigger automation
plan_state: draft
scope_constraints: Create an Airtable pattern where a specific lifecycle/delete field value on a row triggers an Airtable automation that deletes that same row. This closes the assistant-side delete_records gap by letting ChatGPT mark records for deletion through ordinary update operations while Airtable performs the actual deletion through an owner-configured automation.
exact_resume_goal: Review promoted idea and decide whether to execute, refine, or retire it.
why_current_task_matters: This reduces manual cleanup friction during schema resets, stale-row cleanup, duplicate-task cleanup, and future large Airtable migrations where the connector can update records but cannot delete records directly.
next_recommended_action: Review this promoted idea-derived plan and break it into Work Items if still useful.

idea_title: Create Airtable row-delete trigger automation
idea_detail: Create an Airtable pattern where a specific lifecycle/delete field value on a row triggers an Airtable automation that deletes that same row. This closes the assistant-side delete_records gap by letting ChatGPT mark records for deletion through ordinary update operations while Airtable performs the actual deletion through an owner-configured automation.
why_it_matters: This reduces manual cleanup friction during schema resets, stale-row cleanup, duplicate-task cleanup, and future large Airtable migrations where the connector can update records but cannot delete records directly.

fields.event_summary:
idea_id: IDEA-20260423-GEM-ATTACHMENT-IOC-PROVENANCE
idea_title: Gemini attachment review must state exact IOC provenance
idea_detail: Add a reusable Gemini workflow rule for report-driven IOC expansion/removal: when the analyst uploads incident documents and asks Gemini to add missing IOCs or remove incident-localized ones, Gemini must enumerate the files it actually reviewed, distinguish analyst-supplied IOCs from report-derived IOCs, and give short provenance notes for each added or removed indicator family. If only a subset of attachments was reviewed, Gemini should say so plainly instead of implying full review coverage.
why_it_matters: This transcript showed a support-proof gap: analyst note said 5 documents were attached, but Gemini claimed review while naming only 3 and then changed the IOC set without visible per-file grounding. Preserving this as a durable rule helps future Gemini evidence review and repair work.
related_area: Gemini
status: promoted_to_plan
notes: Derived from 2026-04-23 live IOC parsing / pivot transcript. Candidate downstream targets: support-proof / citation lane, upload/attachment honesty lane, and prime-agent response contract guidance.
